edges.description: AADC catalyzes monoamine neurotransmitter synthesis from aromatic amino acid precursors.
Monoamine synthesis failure lowers downstream CSF metabolites and raises precursors.
Dopamine deficiency disrupts basal-ganglia motor function.
intermediate_mechanisms: dopamine deficiency in basal-ganglia motor circuits

edges.description: Peripheral and central monoamine deficiency contributes to autonomic symptoms.
Early combined monoamine deficiency disrupts neurodevelopment.
intermediate_mechanisms: early serotonin and catecholamine deficiency during neurodevelopment

edges.description: Reduced dopamine metabolism lowers CSF homovanillic acid.
Reduced serotonin metabolism lowers CSF 5-hydroxyindoleacetic acid.
Dopamine deficiency can disinhibit prolactin secretion.
AADC deficiency commonly includes ocular motor manifestations including ptosis.
Motor-system dysfunction can include reduced tendon reflexes.
intermediate_mechanisms: abnormal motor tone and reflex control

edges.description: Basal-ganglia and motor-circuit dysfunction can manifest as limb hypertonia.
Central motor dysfunction can manifest as Babinski sign.
Movement-disorder circuitry can rarely manifest as tremor.
Chronic neuromotor impairment can contribute to joint contractures.
intermediate_mechanisms: chronic abnormal motor tone and limited mobility

edges.description: Dysautonomia and neuromotor dysfunction can manifest as constipation.
Gastrointestinal autonomic dysfunction can rarely manifest as diarrhea.
Autonomic and neuromotor gastrointestinal dysfunction can contribute to reflux.
Early serotonin and catecholamine deficiency is associated with behavioral disorders.
Neurodevelopmental monoamine deficiency can include autistic behavior.
Non-motor neurobehavioral manifestations include irritability.
Neurotransmitter imbalance and seizures can be accompanied by EEG abnormalities.
Neurodevelopmental and motor impairment can contribute to swallowing difficulty.
Chronic feeding difficulty and neurometabolic disease can contribute to impaired growth.
intermediate_mechanisms: chronic feeding difficulty and neurometabolic disease

edges.source: Dopamine agonist and MAO inhibitor pharmacotherapy

edges.description: Decreased CSF HVA reports the dopamine-metabolite arm of the AADC deficiency CSF signature.
Decreased CSF 5-HIAA reports the serotonin-metabolite arm of the AADC deficiency CSF signature.
Elevated 3-OMD reports precursor shunting in the AADC deficiency CSF signature.
Reduced plasma AADC activity reports the primary DDC enzyme deficiency.

evidence.snippet: All patients presented distinct extraneurological symptoms, such as hypersalivation, hyperhidrosis, nasal congestion, sleep disturbances and hypoglycaemia.

evidence.snippet: autonomic symptoms such as excessive sweating, nasal congestion and profuse nasal, and oropharyngeal secretions, were common in our patients.

evidence.snippet: HP:0003487 | Babinski sign | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: Loss of AADC activity decreases synthesis of dopamine, serotonin, norepinephrine, and epinephrine, producing central and peripheral monoamine deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Aromatic L-amino acid decarboxylase deficiency
evidence.snippet: impaired synthesis of dopamine, noradrenaline, adrenaline and serotonin.

evidence.reference_title: Consensus guideline for the diagnosis and treatment of aromatic l-amino acid decarboxylase (AADC) deficiency.
evidence.snippet: leads to a severe combined deficiency of serotonin, dopamine, norepinephrine and epinephrine.

evidence.reference_title: Aromatic L-amino acid decarboxylase deficiency: clinical features, drug therapy and follow-up.
evidence.snippet: resulting in generalized combined deficiency of serotonin, dopamine and catecholamines.

nodes.description: AADC deficiency produces a diagnostic CSF neurotransmitter pattern with low homovanillic acid and 5-hydroxyindoleacetic acid and elevated 3-ortho-methyldopa.

evidence.snippet: In CSF all patients revealed the pattern typical of AADC with decreased concentrations of homovanillic and 5-hydroxyindoleacetic acid and elevated concentration of 3-ortho-methyldopa.

evidence.snippet: Concentrations of L-dopa, 3-methoxytyrosine, and 5-hydroxytryptophan were elevated in CSF, plasma, and urine.

nodes.description: Eladocagene exuparvovec delivers DDC via an adeno-associated viral vector to the putamen, aiming to restore dopamine synthesis and improve motor, developmental, and oculogyric manifestations.

evidence.reference_title: Gene therapy improves motor and mental function of aromatic l-amino acid decarboxylase deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Dystonia disappeared and oculogyric crisis was markedly decreased in all patients.
